breaking.text: Trump backs crypto firms against banks on stablecoin yields, Coinbase surges 14%
Broadcom, Veeva Systems, Okta report after-hours earnings — Broadcom analysts expect $2.03 EPS on $19.18B revenue

summary: U.S. equity markets staged a strong recovery on Wednesday, March 4, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.8% to 6,869.50, the Nasdaq surging 1.3% to 22,807.48, and the Dow adding 238 points as moderating oil prices and resilient economic data eased fears from earlier in the week. Key economic releases bolstered sentiment: ADP private payrolls beat expectations at 63,000 jobs (vs. 50,000 est.), the ISM Non-Manufacturing PMI held at 53.5, and the Fed Beige Book showed slight-to-moderate growth in seven districts. Technology stocks led the recovery with Micron and AMD each jumping over 5%, while Amazon gained 3.3% and Coinbase surged 14% after Trump publicly backed crypto firms against banks. Investors now look ahead to after-hours earnings from Broadcom, Veeva Systems, and Okta, with Friday's nonfarm payrolls report and the March 18 FOMC meeting as the next major catalysts.

highlights.text: South Korea's Kospi plunges over 12% amid escalating Middle East war fears, worst day in decades
Micron and AMD each jump 5%+; Amazon gains 3.3% as chip and consumer discretionary sectors lead
Fed Beige Book shows mixed picture: 7 districts with slight-to-moderate growth but 5 districts flat or declining
Coinbase surges 14% as Bitcoin recovers to $73,092; Trump publicly backs crypto firms against banks on stablecoin regulation
ADP private payrolls beat at 63K vs 50K est.; ISM Services PMI steady at 53.5 with cooling prices-paid component
S&P 500 rises 0.8% to 6,869.50 as tech rebound and easing oil prices lift Wall Street
